title: Does the cyclic group C_1801 admit a (1801,225,28)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Lean would currently seal only the 197-multiplier orbit-reduction theorem slice. The publication blocker is earlier and mathematical: the exact 72-class orbit-kernel system still needs a row-closing contradiction or construction, so formalization is optional archival polish rather than the next stop condition.
micro_paper_assessment: The target remains a viable micro-paper row if settled. This audit upgrades the packet from a conditional reduction to an exact structural theorem slice via Baumert-Gordon Theorem 3.2, but it is still not near-paper because the decisive 72-class obstruction is missing.
family_affinity: Residual cyclic-row analysis via prime multiplier normalization and exact full-group orbit-scheme compression.
generalization_signal: The normalization-to-exact-multiplier and orbit-compression template looks reusable, but the unresolved 72-class obstruction appears arithmetic-specific to the order-25 cyclotomic scheme for v = 1801.
strongest_honest_claim: Any cyclic (1801,225,28) difference set can, by Baumert-Gordon Theorem 3.2 and translation normalization, be put into exact 197-invariant form; since ord_1801(197)=25, the normalized set is a union of exactly nine nonzero 25-element multiplier orbits, reducing the selected row to an exact 72-class reduced-kernel system.
paper_title_hint: A 197-Multiplier Orbit Reduction for the Cyclic (1801,225,28) Difference-Set Problem
